title: tabs CRUD tRPC endpoints
description: Implement `tabs.listByPage`, `tabs.create`, `tabs.rename`, `tabs.reorder`, `tabs.delete` tRPC procedures, mirroring the sections/pages permission model.
acceptanceCriteria: `tabs.listByPage` (protectedProcedure query) accepts `{ pageId }` and returns tabs ordered by `ordem`
`tabs.create` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ pageId, titulo }` and assigns the next `ordem`
`tabs.rename` and `tabs.reorder` follow the same pattern as pages.reorder, scoped to one page
`tabs.delete` cascades to `blocks` belonging to the tab
All procedures accessible to both `admin` and `editor` roles

steps.details: Mirror `pages.rename`/`pages.reorder` logic, scoped by `pageId` instead of `sectionId`.
Cascade-delete blocks belonging to the tab (consistent with the deletion strategy chosen in TASK-18).

steps.description: Merge into root router and test
steps.details: Add `tabs: tabsRouter` to the root router. Test full CRUD as `editor`; test that deleting a tab removes its blocks.

technicalNotes: This router is structurally identical to `pages.ts` minus slug handling — consider extracting a small shared helper for the reorder-by-parent pattern to avoid triplicated logic across sections/pages/tabs.
